string.text: Anvil & Hammer - Cavalry & Morale Battle AI
Master toggle. When disabled, this mod no longer changes any battle behavior.
Write detailed runtime info to the log file for troubleshooting. Usually keep this off.
Command only your army; enemy forces fall back to vanilla/RBM. The one exception is formation-level morale, which always affects both sides. Default off (both sides affected).
Organize the army into 7 role formations (main inf / flank inf / archers / horse archers / left light cav / right light cav / heavy cav) as the base for unified command. Default on.

A unified commander directs each formation to run anvil-and-hammer tactics (infantry hold, archer/horse-archer skirmish, light cavalry screen/flank, timed heavy cavalry charge). Requires Auto Formation. Default on.

React to unexpected threats on their own: infantry form a shield wall against cavalry, archers/horse archers fall back, reserve cavalry wheel about to counter-charge. Requires Unified Battle Command. Default on.

When on, formations you command yourself are not overridden by unified command. Keep this off when running RTS Camera, or formations it takes over may stand idle. Default off (the mod runs tactics throughout).
Show key battle events in the lower-left (heavy cavalry charge, light cavalry cover/flank, infantry outflank, rout/rally, etc.) to help you read the AI. Default on.

Fill each formation's troop-type icon with its team colour in proportion to its morale; the lost part turns dark grey (hold Alt to see the markers, or turn on Always Show Formation Markers below). Shown for both your formations and the enemy's, and drains as a formation nears a mass rout. Default on.
Keep the formation markers (the troop-type icons above each formation) visible at all times, without holding Alt. Default off.

Draw a light-grey trail along every arrow, bolt and javelin in flight, kept clearly visible even zoomed out and from the RTS overview (unlike the vanilla trail, which disappears at a distance). Default on.
How long after a formation routs before it tries to steady and rally. Too short = pulled back the instant it routs; too long = can never recover. Default 20.
The morale a formation is restored to when it rallies. Higher = steadier and less likely to rout again at once. Default 25.
Each rout permanently lowers the morale a unit can later recover to (it grows brittle); past a point it collapses for good and no longer rallies.
Each rout multiplies the morale-recovery cap by this factor (lower = collapses for good sooner). Set to 1 for no fragility. Default 0.55.
When your troops rout temporarily, stop the game from declaring defeat too early: routers that can still recover do not leave the field at once. Fully collapsed units and the enemy are not protected.
Make casualties fall more in the pursuit and less in the head-on fight: hits on fleeing or rear-facing enemies deal more, standing toe-to-toe deals less.
Damage multiplier when standing toe-to-toe. Lower = slower kills head-on. Default 0.65.
Damage multiplier when hitting a fleeing enemy. Higher = more thorough pursuit kills. Default 1.7.
Damage multiplier when hitting a (non-fleeing) enemy from behind. Default 1.5.
Hits on a fleeing enemy deal lethal damage outright (one-shot). Aggressive; off by default.
Damage multiplier when hitting a (non-fleeing) enemy from the side. Direction is judged by the enemy formation's facing. Default 1.0 (no change).
Damage multiplier when flank infantry, light cavalry or heavy cavalry strike an enemy formation from behind (replaces the generic rear multiplier; these roles excel at getting around). Default 1.8.
Damage multiplier when flank infantry, light cavalry or heavy cavalry strike an enemy formation from the side (replaces the generic side multiplier). Default 1.3.
Scale Cavalry Impact by Mount Tier
High-speed cavalry collision (impact) damage scales with mount tier: light cavalry lower, heavy cavalry higher. Multiplied on top of the direction multiplier. Default on.
Impact damage multiplier for a light cavalry charge. Light cavalry carries less momentum. Default 0.8.
Impact damage multiplier for a heavy cavalry charge. Heavy cavalry carries great momentum. Default 1.7.

A heavy cavalry charge knocks frontal enemies to the ground, keeping momentum to punch through the line (not halted by standing soldiers, less slowdown). Reflects heavy momentum; off, cavalry is more easily stopped after impact. Only your heavy cavalry, and only knocks down enemies. Default on.
Damage multiplier for hits from bows, crossbows and thrown weapons (arrows, bolts, javelins). Lower = ranged weapons deal less damage. Default 0.8.
Fine-tune speed by role: flank infantry faster to get around, light cavalry and horse archers faster to maneuver, heavy cavalry a touch slower. Off = vanilla speeds. Default on.
Movement-speed multiplier for flank infantry (to get around to the enemy's side/rear). Default 1.25.
Charge-speed multiplier for light cavalry. Default 1.1.
Charge-speed multiplier for horse archers (to help them kite). Default 1.15.
Charge-speed multiplier for heavy cavalry. Heavy armor moves slower. Default 0.8.
A formation routs as a whole once its pressure exceeds this. Lower = easier mass collapse. Default 30.
How much a formation's pressure fades each second. Higher = fades faster, harder to keep it collapsing, recovers sooner after routing. Default 6.
Count being volleyed by arrows and javelins toward rout pressure (shield hits, body hits, and arrows landing nearby all count). Default on.

How strongly being shot at saps a formation toward a mass rout. 100% is the (already reduced) default; lower it if arrows rattle your troops too much, raise it to make massed archery break formations faster. Set 0% to remove rout pressure from fire entirely.
Count enemy cavalry charging in at speed toward rout pressure (closer and faster = more intimidating). Default on.
Main infantry forms up against cavalry
Main infantry (anvil) has the enemy locked, holding the front
Flank infantry forms up against cavalry
Flank infantry strikes the enemy's side and rear
Archers fall back from the cavalry on them
Horse archers fall back from the cavalry on them
Left light cavalry wheels to meet the charging cavalry
Left light cavalry rides to cover our infantry
Left light cavalry strikes the enemy's left
Right light cavalry wheels to meet the charging cavalry
Right light cavalry rides to cover our infantry
Right light cavalry strikes the enemy's right
Heavy cavalry wheels to meet the charging cavalry
Heavy cavalry (hammer) launches the decisive charge!
